title: philotheca brucei subsp. brevifolia
intro: Philotheca brucei subsp. brevifolia, also known as the dwarf mock orange, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Philotheca brucei subsp. brevifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and promote a healthy immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
